page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual machine featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture. This single-core, single-threaded processor operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 1.0 GB of system memory and includes 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, scoring 3294.99, which places it in the top 10% of tested systems. However, because it is limited to a single shared vCPU, its multi-core stress-ng performance is weak, matching the single-core score and landing in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is generally constrained; read-only small block bandwidth is poor at 151.27 GB/sec, and large block read bandwidth is weak at 36.16 GB/sec, though large block write bandwidth reaches an average tier of 29.11 GB/sec. Passmark metrics reflect these limitations, with poor scores in database operations, physics, and overall memory performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents an entry-level tier that provides modern AMD Genoa architecture features, such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, at a low resource density. The shared CPU allocation and 1.0 GB memory limit its suitability for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripts, low-traffic static web serving, or small-scale development and testing environments where high multi-core throughput and large memory capacities are not required.
description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-amd is an entry-level virtual machine from the High Performance family, powered by a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 1.0 GB of RAM, 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally weak or poor due to the single shared vCPU and low memory capacity. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. This server is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for basic utility scripts, small-scale development, and low-traffic static web serving.

tagline: A budget-friendly single-core virtual server featuring modern AMD EPYC Genoa architecture and local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.
